curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 19 units, 19 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Determine molecular masses using colligative property measurements
Differentiate between ideal and non-ideal solution behaviors based on vapor pressure deviations
Evaluate factors affecting solubility and express concentration in various units
Apply the Nernst equation to predict electrode potential under non-standard conditions
Distinguish between galvanic and electrolytic cells based on energy conversion processes
Analyze mechanisms of corrosion and evaluate efficiency in fuel cells and batteries
Derive and utilize integrated rate laws to determine reaction order from concentration-time data
Explain the effect of temperature on reaction rates using Arrhenius parameters and collision theory
Identify factors influencing reaction kinetics including catalysts, surface area, and reactant nature
Correlate electronic configuration with magnetic and catalytic properties of d-block elements
Compare the chemical behavior and oxidation states of lanthanoids versus actinoids
Assess the significance of transition metals in forming colored complexes and alloys

topics: Properties, Measurement and Uncertainty in Measurement
Atomic Models and Bohr's Model for Hydrogen Atom

Bending a glass tube

practicals.title: Determination of boiling point of an organic compound
practicals.details: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

practicals.title: Crystallization of Alum, Copper Sulphate, or Benzoic Acid
practicals.details: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances. Impure sample purification.

practicals.title: Determination of pH using fruit juices/known solutions with universal indicator/pH paper
Comparing the pH of strong and weak acids of same concentration
Study pH change in titration of a strong base using universal indicator
Study the pH change by common-ion effect for weak acids and bases
Volumetric Analysis (Titration)
practicals.details: Evaluation Scheme Component. Includes preparation of standard solutions and determination of strength.

practicals.title: Salt Analysis
practicals.details: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: Pb2+, Cu2+, Al3+, Fe3+, Mn2+, Ni2+, Zn2+, Co2+, Ca2+, Sr2+, Ba2+, Mg2+, NH4+; Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, NO3 -, NO2 -, Cl-, Br-, I-, SO4 2-, PO4 3-, CH3COO-). Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds.

practicals.title: Study shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions
practicals.details: Chemical Equilibrium. Study effect of concentration change on equilibrium position.

practicals.title: Study shift in equilibrium between [Co(H2O)6] 2+ and chloride ions
practicals.details: Chemical Equilibrium. Study effect of concentration change on equilibrium position.

practicals.title: Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid / Sodium carbonate
Determination of strength of NaOH by titrating against standard Oxalic acid
Determination of strength of HCl by titrating against standard Sodium Carbonate solution
Preparation of lyophilic sol (starch, egg albumin, gum) and lyophobic sol (aluminum hydroxide, ferric hydroxide)
Dialysis of prepared sols
Study role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing emulsion of different oils
Effect of concentration and temperature on rate of reaction between Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid
Study reaction rates: Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide or Potassium Iodate and Sodium Sulphate
Enthalpy of dissolution (Copper Sulphate/Potassium Nitrate) or neutralization (HCl/NaOH)
Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with change in electrolyte concentration
Separation of pigments from leaves/flowers by paper chromatography and determination of Rf values
Preparation of double salt (Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum)
practicals.details: Preparation of Inorganic Compounds. Class XII Practical.

practicals.title: Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
Preparation of Organic Compound (Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol dye)
practicals.details: Preparation of Organic Compounds. Class XII Practical.

practicals.title: Tests for functional groups (Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic, amino)
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples and foodstuffs
practicals.details: Detection in given foodstuffs. Class XII Practical.

practicals.title: Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titration against Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate

Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: Pb2+, Cu2+, Al3+, Fe3+, Mn2+, Ni2+, Zn2+, Co2+, Ca2+, Sr2+, Ba2+, Mg2+, NH4+; Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, NO3 -, NO2 -, Cl-, Br-, I-, SO4 2-, PO4 3-, CH3COO-)
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: NH4+; Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, Cl-, CH3COO-)
practicals.details: Qualitative Analysis. Class XII Practical for Visually Challenged.

practicals.title: Detection of Nitrogen in organic compound
Detection of Halogen in organic compound
practicals.details: Class XII Practical for Visually Challenged.

projects.title: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study of the methods of purification of water
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride in drinking water
projects.details: Study causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit depending upon regional variation.

projects.title: Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and effect of addition of Sodium carbonate
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
class_xi_suggested_projects: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of the methods of purification of water

Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water and study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit (if any)
Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
class_xii_suggested_projects: Study of the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study of quantity of casein present in different samples of milk
Preparation of soybean milk and its comparison with the natural milk with respect to curd formation, effect of temperature, etc.
Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time, etc.)
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of following materials: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom)
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
class_xi_suggested_projects: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of the methods of purification of water

Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water and study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit (if any)
Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
class_xii_suggested_projects: Study of the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study of quantity of casein present in different samples of milk
Preparation of soybean milk and its comparison with the natural milk with respect to curd formation, effect of temperature, etc.
Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time, etc.)
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of following materials: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom)
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques -> Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Equip learners with tools to understand the working of chemistry, develop conceptual foundations for real-life application, and enable representation at macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic levels.
competencies: Apply mole concept and stoichiometric calculations to determine percentage composition and molar masses
Represent chemical phenomena using laws of combination and Dalton's atomic theory across different scales
Identify patterns in measurement uncertainty and properties of matter

competency_groups.title: Structure of Atom
competency_groups.description: Develop understanding of atomic structure through historical models and quantum mechanical principles to explain chemical behavior.
competencies: Trace the development from sub-atomic particle discovery to Bohr's model for hydrogen atom
Apply quantum mechanical model concepts to describe electronic arrangements in atoms

competency_groups.title: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify elements based on periodic laws, electronic configurations, and identify trends across s-, p-, d-, f- blocks.
competencies: Utilize modern periodic law and nomenclature rules for elements with atomic number > 100
Correlate electronic configuration types (s, p, d, f) with element classification in the periodic table
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure
competency_groups.description: Foster ability to analyze bonding mechanisms using Kossel-Lewis, VBT, MOT, and VSEPR theories to predict molecular structures.
competencies: Differentiate between ionic/electrovalent bonds and covalent interactions via Lewis approach
Predict bond parameters and molecular geometry using hybridization concepts and VSEPR theory

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics
competency_groups.description: Prepare learners to analyze energy changes, spontaneity, and equilibrium in chemical processes using thermodynamic terms.
competencies: Calculate enthalpy changes for various reaction types and interpret calorimetry data (∆U and ∆H)
Evaluate the spontaneity of reactions based on Gibbs Energy change and equilibrium conditions

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics Competencies
competency_groups.description: Core competencies for Thermodynamics.
competencies: Understand the core concepts of Thermodynamics.
Apply key ideas from Thermodynamics to solve curriculum-aligned problems.
Analyze relationships between major concepts in Thermodynamics.

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies derived from the Equilibrium chapter covering dynamic equilibrium, thermodynamic relationships, and factors affecting equilibria.
competencies: Analyze dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes
Relate reaction quotient (Q), equilibrium constant (K), and Gibbs energy to predict spontaneity
Evaluate the impact of external factors on chemical equilibria

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies derived from the Redox Reactions chapter focusing on electron transfer and oxidation states.
competencies: Identify redox reactions based on electron transfer mechanisms
Determine oxidation numbers to balance chemical equations
Explain electrode processes in electrochemical cells

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Principles Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies derived from the Organic Chemistry chapter covering nomenclature, isomerism, and reaction mechanisms.
competencies: Classify organic compounds and apply IUPAC nomenclature rules
Distinguish between different types of isomers in organic structures
Analyze fundamental concepts underlying organic reaction mechanisms
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbons Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies derived from the Hydrocarbons chapter addressing classification, properties of aromatic compounds, and safety.
competencies: Differentiate between alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes based on structure and reactivity
Identify characteristics of aromatic hydrocarbons
Assess carcinogenicity and toxicity associated with specific hydrocarbon classes

competency_groups.title: Solutions and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Analyze the properties of solutions, including concentration expressions, solubility factors, vapor pressure behavior in ideal and non-ideal systems, and the application of colligative properties for molar mass determination.
competencies: Determine molecular masses using colligative property measurements
Differentiate between ideal and non-ideal solution behaviors based on vapor pressure deviations
Evaluate factors affecting solubility and express concentration in various units

competency_groups.title: Electrochemical Systems and Applications
competency_groups.description: Examine the principles of electrochemical cells, calculate cell potentials using the Nernst equation, understand conductance in electrolytic solutions, and analyze practical applications like batteries and corrosion.
competencies: Apply the Nernst equation to predict electrode potential under non-standard conditions
Distinguish between galvanic and electrolytic cells based on energy conversion processes
Analyze mechanisms of corrosion and evaluate efficiency in fuel cells and batteries

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics and Reaction Dynamics
competency_groups.description: Investigate the rates of chemical reactions, derive integrated rate equations for different reaction orders, explore temperature dependence via activation energy, and apply collision theory to explain reaction mechanisms.
competencies: Derive and utilize integrated rate laws to determine reaction order from concentration-time data
Explain the effect of temperature on reaction rates using Arrhenius parameters and collision theory
Identify factors influencing reaction kinetics including catalysts, surface area, and reactant nature

competency_groups.title: d-and f-Block Elements: Properties and Trends
competency_groups.description: Explore the electronic configurations, general properties, and periodic trends of transition elements (d-block) and inner transition elements (f-block), including their important compounds and industrial applications.
competencies: Correlate electronic configuration with magnetic and catalytic properties of d-block elements
Compare the chemical behavior and oxidation states of lanthanoids versus actinoids
Assess the significance of transition metals in forming colored complexes and alloys

competency_groups.title: Coordination Chemistry: Structure and Bonding
competency_groups.description: Understand the fundamental concepts of coordination compounds, including nomenclature rules, isomerism types, bonding theories (Valence Bond Theory), and their critical role in biological and industrial systems.
competencies: Apply IUPAC nomenclature conventions to name complex coordination entities accurately
Predict the geometry and magnetic behavior of complexes using Werner's theory and VBT
Distinguish between structural and stereoisomerism in coordination compounds

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes Competencies
competency_groups.description: Understanding the nature of C-X bond, preparation methods, physical properties, chemical reactions, and polyhalogen compounds.
competencies: Analyze the polarity and reactivity of the carbon-halogen (C–X) bond in haloalkanes and haloarenes
Evaluate various laboratory and industrial methods for preparing haloalkanes and haloarenes
Predict chemical reaction outcomes based on functional group transformations

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers Competencies
competency_groups.description: Mastering the structures of functional groups, properties of alcohols and phenols, important commercial alcohols, and ethers.
competencies: Differentiate between the structural characteristics and acidity of alcohols versus phenols
Identify commercially significant alcohols and their specific applications in industry
Determine physical properties and reactivity patterns distinguishing ethers from other oxygenated compounds

competency_groups.title: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids Competencies
competency_groups.description: Exploring carbonyl group structures, preparation methods, physical properties, chemical reactions, and uses of aldehydes, ketones, and carboxylic acids.
competencies: Compare the reactivity differences between aldehydes and ketones based on their structural features
Synthesize strategies for preparing carboxylic acids from various precursor compounds
Correlate physical properties with chemical behavior in carbonyl-containing functional groups

competency_groups.title: Amines Competencies
competency_groups.description: Focusing on amine nomenclature, preparation methods, physical properties, chemical reactions, and the synthesis of aromatic compounds via diazonium salts.
competencies: Classify amines based on structure and apply IUPAC nomenclature rules accurately
Design synthetic pathways for preparing primary, secondary, and tertiary amines
Utilize diazonium salt reactions to synthesize complex aromatic compounds

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules Competencies
competency_groups.description: Investigating the structure and function of proteins, enzymes, vitamins, nucleic acids, hormones, and carbohydrates.
competencies: Explain the hierarchical structural organization of proteins and their biological functions as enzymes
Categorize essential vitamins based on solubility and physiological roles in human health
Analyze the role of nucleic acids and hormones in genetic information transfer and metabolic regulation

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
